claims.text: Continuous integration is the practice of merging all developer working copies to a shared mainline several times a day, with each integration verified by an automated build and automated test suite to detect integration errors as quickly as possible.

evidence_spans.text: Continuous integration is the practice of merging all developer working copies to a shared mainline several times a day. Each integration is verified by an automated build and an automated test suite to detect integration errors as quickly as possible.

claims.text: The TDD cycle is red, green, refactor: write a failing test, write the minimal code to make it pass, then refactor while keeping the tests green.

evidence_spans.text: The cycle is red, green, refactor: write a failing test, write the minimal code to make it pass, then refactor while keeping the tests green.

claims.text: Pull requests should be kept under about four hundred lines to help reviewers stay focused and find more defects.

evidence_spans.text: Keeping a change under about four hundred lines helps reviewers stay focused and find more defects.

claims.text: An error budget is the amount of unreliability a service is allowed to accumulate before the team must stop shipping features and focus on reliability.

evidence_spans.text: An error budget is the amount of unreliability a service is allowed to accumulate before the team must stop shipping features and focus on reliability.

claims.text: Four phase-gate validators enforce the gates automatically in the programme SDLC method.

evidence_spans.text: Four phase-gate validators enforce the gates automatically.

claims.text: A canary deployment releases the new version to a small subset of users before rolling it out to everyone; if the canary shows elevated errors the team rolls back before most users are affected.

evidence_spans.text: A canary deployment releases the new version to a small subset of users before rolling it out to everyone. If the canary shows elevated errors the team rolls back before most users are affected.

claims.text: Release management is the process of planning, scheduling, and controlling the movement of a software release through its stages to production, with the goal of delivering value safely and predictably.

evidence_spans.text: Release management is the process of planning, scheduling, and controlling the movement of a software release through its stages to production. Its goal is to deliver value safely and predictably.

claims.text: The single-branch trunk model is documented in trunk-based.md, which describes trunk-based development as a branching model where developers collaborate on a single branch called trunk.

evidence_spans.text: Trunk-based development is a branching model where developers collaborate on a single branch called trunk.

claims.text: Semantic versioning is explained in release-management.md: it assigns a three-part version number of the form major dot minor dot patch, where the major version increases for incompatible changes, the minor version for backward-compatible features, and the patch version for backward-compatible bug fixes.

evidence_spans.text: Semantic versioning assigns a three-part version number of the form major dot minor dot patch. The major version increases for incompatible changes, the minor version for backward-compatible features, and the patch version for backward-compatible bug fixes.
